name: Monstera obliqua 'Yasuni Form'
aboutText: The Monstera obliqua 'Yasuni Form' is a highly sought-after botanical treasure hailing from the rainforest understories of Yasuní, Ecuador. This delicate epiphytic climber is prized for its extreme fenestration, where the leaf surface area is dominated by intricate holes rather than solid lamina. As a unique locality form, it maintains a graceful, narrow silhouette that differentiates it from the more common, broader Monstera obliqua variants. Its requirement for high humidity and stable, warm environments makes it a challenging but rewarding specimen for the advanced aroid enthusiast.

fieldNotes.title: Yasuní: The Most Biodiverse Place on Earth
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: Yasuní National Park in Ecuador has been described as containing more tree species in a single hectare than exist in all of North America — a claim that regularly provokes scepticism and just as regularly survives scrutiny. Material from the Yasuní locality carries the full weight of that ecological context. Ultra rare, top price tier: appropriate for authenticated obliqua from one of the world's most documented biodiversity hotspots. Whether you are the kind of collector for whom locality matters as much as the plant itself determines whether this specific form is worth pursuing over other obliqua material.
